title: Discover
subtitle: Entering by the threshold
intro: Before journeying, one must know where one is entering. This door gives the context: what Sufism is — and is not —, how it was born, how it spread, how it organised itself into spiritual families.

sections.title: What is Sufism
sections.subtitle: The essence of the path
sections.preview: An inner dimension of Islam, a path of love and knowledge, a science of the heart — etymology, the hadith of Gabriel, misconceptions to move beyond.

sections.title: The great misconceptions
sections.subtitle: What Sufism is not
sections.preview: Not a folklore, not a sect, not a mere religious emotion — seven clichés to move beyond in order to enter the reality of the path.

sections.title: The hadith of Gabriel
sections.subtitle: Islām, īmān, iḥsān
sections.preview: The founding prophetic saying that inscribes Sufism as the dimension of excellence of Islam.

sections.title: The origin of the word “Sufi”
sections.subtitle: Ṣūf, ṣafāʾ, ṣuffa — three etymologies
sections.preview: The coarse wool of the ascetics, the purity of the heart, the bench of the Prophet's Companions. Three roots, a single face.

sections.title: The essential lexicon
sections.subtitle: The great words of the path
sections.preview: Forty terms of Sufism — tasawwuf, qalb, dhikr, nafs, fanāʾ, mahabba, iḥsān — with their vocalised Arabic spelling and their meaning.

sections.title: History
sections.subtitle: Twelve centuries of an inner path
sections.preview: From the first ascetics of Iraq to contemporary Sufism — ten periods for twelve centuries.

sections.title: Currents & orders
sections.subtitle: The organised paths
sections.preview: Fourteen great paths — Qādiriyya, Shādhiliyya, Naqshbandiyya, Mevleviyya, Chishtiyya, Tijāniyya…

sections.title: Spiritual geography
sections.subtitle: The lands of Sufism
sections.preview: Nine lands, nine flavours: Baghdad, Bukhara, Andalusia, Konya, Shiraz, Ajmer, Touba…
